description_long: This house features a steeply pitched gable roof with dark shingles and a prominent front-facing dormer. The exterior walls are clad in red brick on the lower level and vertical beige siding on the upper gable ends, creating a visually appealing contrast. The front facade includes a covered porch with white railings and columns, accessible by a set of concrete steps. Windows are symmetrically arranged, with arched windows in the gables and rectangular windows on the main floor, including a smaller dormer window on one side. The foundation is concrete, and the house sits slightly elevated above ground level. Architectural details such as the decorative trim and the porch's balustrade add to its traditional suburban charm.
description: A traditional brick and siding house with a steep gable roof, covered front porch, and symmetrical window arrangement.
description_view_0: Side view showing the tall gable end with an arched window and vertical siding above a brick wall.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the covered porch with white railings, central entryway, and symmetrical gable with arched window.
description_view_2: Angled side view displaying the porch, a large rectangular window, and the main gable with arched window.
description_view_3: Rear view showing a plain brick wall with no windows or doors, and the large expanse of the gable roof.
attribute_window_arrangement: symmetrical rows with arched gable windows and rectangular main floor windows
attribute_entryway_design: Central entry with covered porch, white railings, columns, and concrete steps
